# Feature Title

## Summary

Describe the feature in one or two concrete paragraphs.

## User Goal

Describe what the user is trying to accomplish and why it matters.

## Scope

List the behavior included in this feature.

## Non-Goals

List related behavior that is explicitly out of scope.

## Beginner Behavior

Describe the Simple Mode behavior and defaults.

## Pro Behavior

Describe Pro Mode behavior where relevant. If not relevant, state that no Pro Mode distinction is required.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Criterion 1.
- Criterion 2.
- Criterion 3.

## Given/When/Then Examples

- Given a starting condition, when the user or system acts, then an observable result occurs.

## Edge Cases

- Edge case 1.
- Edge case 2.

## Data Model Implications

Describe semantic model or metadata changes.

## Storage Implications

Describe `.dreamjotter` package implications and whether data is canonical or derived.

## Command Implications

Describe commands required for mutation, including undo or snapshot expectations where relevant.

## UI Implications

Describe UI requirements without coupling core behavior to UI frameworks.

## Platform Implications

Describe macOS, iPadOS, and iOS implications.

## Cross-Platform Implications

Describe later Linux, Windows, Android, or portable-core implications.

## Testability Notes

Describe future unit, integration, fixture, or UI test coverage.

## Security/Privacy Notes

Describe local data, AI, destructive action, or privacy considerations.

## Open Questions

- Question 1.
